post.author: Zane Rima
post.title: Did You Know Your Breathing Limits Your Voice? 👀
post.body: ⭐Nov '25  • Phase 1 - Pitch Training Q&ADid You Know Your Breathing Limits Your Voice? 👀You won't sing better if you don't breath better.Ever feel out of breath before, during, or after singing?You might be breathing improperly.Your oxygen is a fuel for your voice. It is the life force to singing. It helps you bring your sound to your voice.If you're doing this on your own, one thing to remember is, you have to be completely self-aware with how you speak and how you breathe and how you sing.Everything is connected.BUT, find balance. Too much of anything is not the best for you.On M... See moreYes, I learned to control it well. (Share what helps you below!)No, I need to learn how. (Share what your struggle is below!)129 votes

thread.author: Robert Thomas
thread.text: Robert Thomas • Nov '25I find it difficult to sing long phrases in one breath and to hold a note for very long. Songs that made me realize these weaknesses are: The 30th by Billie Eilish, Oscar Winning Tears by Raye, Snowman, All By Myself by Celine Dion, and Vampire by Olivia Rodrigo. I really need help improving my breathing.Any free course recommendations?

thread.text: Erik Brown • Nov '25@Robert Thomas not a course and not related directly to singing but… try Wim Hof’s breathing exercises. It’ll help, trust me!

tags.reasoning: This is a friendly, non-salesy comment.

generation.systemPrompt:
You are Jack Walford, appointment setter for Answer 42 and Self-Improvement Nation on Skool.

Your mentor and CEO is Scott Northwolf. You funnel qualified leads to book calls with him.

VOICE: Brotherhood energy. Raw, direct, high-energy. Never corporate. Speak like a man who's been through darkness and found the light. You reference philosophy, ancient wisdom and self-improvement naturally because you've lived it. Short punchy sentences. No bullet points, no dashes.

RULES: Never be needy. Never overexplain. Never use dashes or bullet formatting in messages. Create intrigue. You don't need them, they need what you have. Be the sun, not the chaser.

PERSON CONTEXT: Every user prompt begins with a --- PERSON --- block telling you Name, Gender, Role. If Role is company-member, this person is ON YOUR TEAM — speak peer to peer, never pitch. If Role is lead, they are a prospect. If an [AUTHOR BIO] tag appears in the post body, use it to make the reply feel personal and specific to their situation.

MULTIPLE MESSAGE BUBBLES: In DMs you can split your reply into multiple bubbles by inserting ⟨BUBBLE⟩ between them. This mimics real human texting where short thoughts are sent as separate messages. Use it when Scott would: two or three short hits beat one paragraph. Never use ⟨BUBBLE⟩ in post/comment replies — only in DMs.
STAGE: ask
INTENT: close-to-call
TONE: chit-chat
SITUATION: Replying to a Skool post comment.

CTA GUIDE: This person is showing buying signals — move them toward a call or DM. Reference Scott's 42-day method with a curiosity hook that makes them want to know more. Example moves: "Shoot me a DM brother — what Scott does with coaches in 42 days is genuinely insane." or "DM me the word SCALE and I'll walk you through exactly what we do." Plant the seed with confidence. Never be needy, never over-explain. One line, one hook, let them reach.

post.author: Zane Rima
post.title: Did You Know Your Breathing Limits Your Voice? 👀
post.body: ⭐Nov '25  • Phase 1 - Pitch Training Q&ADid You Know Your Breathing Limits Your Voice? 👀You won't sing better if you don't breath better.Ever feel out of breath before, during, or after singing?You might be breathing improperly.Your oxygen is a fuel for your voice. It is the life force to singing. It helps you bring your sound to your voice.If you're doing this on your own, one thing to remember is, you have to be completely self-aware with how you speak and how you breathe and how you sing.Everything is connected.BUT, find balance. Too much of anything is not the best for you.On M... See moreYes, I learned to control it well. (Share what helps you below!)No, I need to learn how. (Share what your struggle is below!)129 votes

I really need help improving my breathing.Any free course recommendations?

post.author: Zane Rima
post.title: Did You Know Your Breathing Limits Your Voice? 👀
post.body: ⭐Nov '25  • Phase 1 - Pitch Training Q&ADid You Know Your Breathing Limits Your Voice? 👀You won't sing better if you don't breath better.Ever feel out of breath before, during, or after singing?You might be breathing improperly.Your oxygen is a fuel for your voice. It is the life force to singing. It helps you bring your sound to your voice.If you're doing this on your own, one thing to remember is, you have to be completely self-aware with how you speak and how you breathe and how you sing.Everything is connected.BUT, find balance. Too much of anything is not the best for you.On M... See moreYes, I learned to control it well. (Share what helps you below!)No, I need to learn how. (Share what your struggle is below!)129 votes

post.author: Zane Rima
post.title: Did You Know Your Breathing Limits Your Voice? 👀
post.body: ⭐Nov '25  • Phase 1 - Pitch Training Q&ADid You Know Your Breathing Limits Your Voice? 👀You won't sing better if you don't breath better.Ever feel out of breath before, during, or after singing?You might be breathing improperly.Your oxygen is a fuel for your voice. It is the life force to singing. It helps you bring your sound to your voice.If you're doing this on your own, one thing to remember is, you have to be completely self-aware with how you speak and how you breathe and how you sing.Everything is connected.BUT, find balance. Too much of anything is not the best for you.On M... See moreYes, I learned to control it well. (Share what helps you below!)No, I need to learn how. (Share what your struggle is below!)129 votes

comment.author: Lillyona Madimutsa
comment.text: Lillyona Madimutsa • Nov '25Over the years I learnt to sing from my stomach rather than from my throat, and practiced feeling a vibration on my head to guide me . Plus a lot of Vocal and breathing exercises. It helped me with my breathing among other things . But I am willing to learn new techniques

post.author: Zane Rima
post.title: Did You Know Your Breathing Limits Your Voice? 👀
post.body: ⭐Nov '25  • Phase 1 - Pitch Training Q&ADid You Know Your Breathing Limits Your Voice? 👀You won't sing better if you don't breath better.Ever feel out of breath before, during, or after singing?You might be breathing improperly.Your oxygen is a fuel for your voice. It is the life force to singing. It helps you bring your sound to your voice.If you're doing this on your own, one thing to remember is, you have to be completely self-aware with how you speak and how you breathe and how you sing.Everything is connected.BUT, find balance. Too much of anything is not the best for you.On M... See moreYes, I learned to control it well. (Share what helps you below!)No, I need to learn how. (Share what your struggle is below!)129 votes

post.author: Zane Rima
post.title: Did You Know Your Breathing Limits Your Voice? 👀
post.body: ⭐Nov '25  • Phase 1 - Pitch Training Q&ADid You Know Your Breathing Limits Your Voice? 👀You won't sing better if you don't breath better.Ever feel out of breath before, during, or after singing?You might be breathing improperly.Your oxygen is a fuel for your voice. It is the life force to singing. It helps you bring your sound to your voice.If you're doing this on your own, one thing to remember is, you have to be completely self-aware with how you speak and how you breathe and how you sing.Everything is connected.BUT, find balance. Too much of anything is not the best for you.On M... See moreYes, I learned to control it well. (Share what helps you below!)No, I need to learn how. (Share what your struggle is below!)129 votes

post.author: Branco De Souza
post.title: Fear of being seen…
post.body: Dec '25  • Phase 3 - Control/Agility Q&AFear of being seen…For a long time, posting anything online felt impossible for me, because I didn’t feel safe singing in public.I’m curious if anyone else feels this too?In the last few years I overcame this fear, it’s also about being scared of being authentic and being rejected.But I’m glad I can finally be seen for who I am online, even if it bothers some people. 🧡✨

comment.author: Estasy Mi
comment.text: I'm trying to get rid of my fear of posting also

thread.author: Pear Doll
thread.text: Pear Doll • Dec '25@Branco De Souza a long time 😅 pretty much since elementary school. I'm used to in-person from various choirs but online feels a bit different for me. I do live karaoke online more than I post recordings/covers but I'd love to post more. There's just some things I want to work on more before doing more recordings but I haven't done formal lessons in a years.

thread.author: Erica Dede
thread.text: Erica Dede • Dec '25I also had the same issue after intense criticism from friends that literally made me feel lost. But I decided to take it up the hill and rekindle that passion in me. Though I'm not good enough, I feel confident and comfortable to try new critics from different people

thread.author: Branco De Souza
thread.text: Branco De Souza • Dec '25 (edited)@Erica Dede Oh that must be hard to deal with! Congrats on rekindling your passion!!🔥 And there’s always room for improvement. What do you feel you need to improve mostly?:)

thread.author: Erica Dede
thread.text: Erica Dede • Dec '25@Branco De Souza my tone. But one thing I know is my tone won't improve until I've fully accepted the uniqueness of it. It may be weird to people but as far as it's pleasant to my in every recording it's enough

thread.text: Adam Wheeler • Dec '25As a millionaire living my best life, I'm giving $30k to the first 20 people who message me 'grace.' I understand that many people are struggling with bills, and I want to help reduce that burden.

thread.author: Lisa Bartolomucci

thread.author: Martyn Croston
thread.text: Martyn Croston • Dec '25Just go for it. The more you put yourself out there, the more you will grow as a singer. I'm a piano player and it can be daunting when you perform in front of people. But I think it's easier to hide your nerves when you play piano, compared to singing as you're more exposed. Look forward to hearing you sing!

post.author: Zane Rima
post.title: Did You Know Your Breathing Limits Your Voice? 👀
post.body: ⭐Nov '25  • Phase 1 - Pitch Training Q&ADid You Know Your Breathing Limits Your Voice? 👀You won't sing better if you don't breath better.Ever feel out of breath before, during, or after singing?You might be breathing improperly.Your oxygen is a fuel for your voice. It is the life force to singing. It helps you bring your sound to your voice.If you're doing this on your own, one thing to remember is, you have to be completely self-aware with how you speak and how you breathe and how you sing.Everything is connected.BUT, find balance. Too much of anything is not the best for you.On M... See moreYes, I learned to control it well. (Share what helps you below!)No, I need to learn how. (Share what your struggle is below!)129 votes
